summary.en: This is the historic soul of Muscat, a vibrant maritime district where the past feels ever-present. The air along the Corniche is thick with the scent of sea salt and frankincense wafting from the ancient souq.
A world away from the ancient bustle of Muttrah, this area represents the modern, spiritual face of Oman, defined by the breathtaking scale and serene elegance of the Sultan Qaboos Grand Mosque.

description.en: One of the oldest bazaars in the Arabian world, this sprawling, covered market is a maze of narrow passageways with vendors selling everything from silver khanjar to precious frankincense.

name.en: 'What Is It?' Statuette at the National Museum of Oman

description.en: A mysterious, small anthropomorphic statuette offering a fascinating and tangible link to Oman's enigmatic pre-Islamic past, located within the National Museum.
The waterfront along the Muttrah Corniche provides a stunning view of the historic port, where traditional dhows bob against a backdrop of the 16th-century Muttrah Fort.

name.en: Chandelier at the Sultan Qaboos Grand Mosque

description.en: A monumental piece of art, this is the second-largest single-piece chandelier in the world, dripping with 600,000 Swarovski crystals and plated with 24-carat gold.
